dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3141 IS: Securing Assurance for Federal Employees Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Warner (for himself, Mr. Van Hollen, Mr. Schumer, Ms. Hirono, Mr. Kaine, Ms. Alsobrooks, Mr. Blumenthal, Ms. Duckworth, Mr. Kim, and Mr. Wyden) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

form.official-title: To prohibit Executive agencies from carrying out a reduction in force, or any similar effort, during any period during which there is a lapse in appropriations, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Securing Assurance for Federal Employees Act or the SAFE Act.

section.header: Prohibition on carrying out a reduction in force during Government shutdown

subsection.text: In this section, the term Executive agency has the meaning given the term in section 105 of title 5, United States Code.

During a period during which there is a lapse in appropriations with respect to an Executive agency, that Executive agency may not use the funds made available by any Act to propose, notice, initiate, execute, implement, or take any other action relating to a reduction in force, or relating to any similar effort, to reduce the number of employees employed by that or another Executive agency.

With respect to any reduction in force (or similar effort) proposed, noticed, initiated, executed, implemented, or otherwise taken by an Executive agency in violation of subsection (b), including such an action taken with respect to a reduction in force (or similar effort) at any time during the period beginning on October 1, 2025, and ending on the day before the date of enactment of this Act, that reduction in force (or similar effort) shall have no force or effect.
Nothing in this section may be construed to affect a voluntary separation payment offered to an employee under section 3523 of title 5, United States Code.
This section shall take effect as if enacted on September 30, 2025.
